## chunk overview
summary: The Aurora K2 is a 75% wireless mechanical keyboard built around a CNC-milled aluminium case and a gasket-mounted polycarbonate plate.
tags: aurora-k2
anchor: #overview

The Aurora K2 is a 75% wireless mechanical keyboard built around a CNC-milled aluminium case and a gasket-mounted polycarbonate plate. It connects over 2.4 GHz (included dongle), Bluetooth 5.3 to three paired devices, or USB-C, and switches between them with a dedicated key. The hot-swap PCB accepts any 3- or 5-pin MX-style switch, so the stock switches are a starting point, not a commitment.

## chunk battery-and-connectivity
summary: A 4000 mAh battery runs the K2 for up to 320 hours with the backlight off, or around 40 hours at full brightness.
anchor: #battery-and-connectivity

A 4000 mAh battery runs the K2 for up to 320 hours with the backlight off, or around 40 hours at full brightness. Charging over USB-C takes 2.5 hours and the keyboard works while charging. On 2.4 GHz the polling rate is 1000 Hz — the same as wired — while Bluetooth polls at 125 Hz, which we recommend for typing rather than gaming.

## chunk in-the-box
summary: Keyboard with keycaps and switches fitted, 2.4 GHz USB-A dongle, 1.8 m braided USB-C cable, keycap and switch puller, spare gaskets, and ISO-DE supplementary…
tags: ghz-usb-a
anchor: #in-the-box

Keyboard with keycaps and switches fitted, 2.4 GHz USB-A dongle, 1.8 m braided USB-C cable, keycap and switch puller, spare gaskets, and ISO-DE supplementary keycap set.

## chunk shipping-and-returns
summary: Orders placed before 14:00 CET ship the same day from our Rotterdam warehouse.
anchor: #shipping-and-returns

Orders placed before 14:00 CET ship the same day from our Rotterdam warehouse. EU shipping is free over €80 (otherwise €5.90); UK and Switzerland €12.90 with duties prepaid. Returns are accepted within 30 days in any condition — yes, including after you've swapped the switches — with a prepaid label for EU customers.

## chunk reviews
summary: "The gasket mount makes it noticeably softer than my old tray-mount board — I typed this review at 2am without waking anyone." — Jonas K., verified purchase…
tags: jonas-k, priya-s, álvaro-m
anchor: #reviews

4.6 /5

Based on 312 verified purchases. 87% would recommend to a friend.

Most praised: typing feel, battery life. Most criticised: weight (it is not a travel board).

"The gasket mount makes it noticeably softer than my old tray-mount board — I typed this review at 2am without waking anyone." — Jonas K., verified purchase, March 2026. "Swapped the Tides for lubed Reefs in ten minutes, no soldering, and the stabilisers came tuned from the factory." — Priya S., verified purchase, February 2026. "Two kilograms. My desk approves, my backpack has filed a complaint." — Álvaro M., verified purchase, April 2026.

## chunk faq
summary: The K2 runs QMK-compatible firmware with VIA support, so every key and layer is remappable in the browser with no software install.
tags: can-i, k2-lp
anchor: #faq

Does the K2 work with macOS, Windows and Linux?

Yes. A physical toggle switches the modifier layout between Mac and Windows/Linux legends; both sets of keycaps are included. Firmware updates ship for all three platforms.

Can I remap keys?

The K2 runs QMK-compatible firmware with VIA support, so every key and layer is remappable in the browser with no software install.

Is there a low-profile version?

Not yet. A low-profile K2 LP is on the public roadmap for late 2026.
